<thought>
The observation shows that there are lava cells (L) directly ahead and to both sides of me. Since I cannot move into any of these dangerous cells, I need to find a safe direction to proceed. Turning left would lead me towards an unknown cell, but turning right could potentially lead me away from the lava.

I will turn right to avoid the immediate danger.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The lava cells (L) are directly ahead and to the left of my current position. Moving forward or to the left would lead me into the lava. Therefore, I should turn right to avoid the danger.

After turning right, I can move forward since the cell to the right is unknown but not currently known to be dangerous.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The lava cell (L) is directly ahead of me. I cannot move forward into it. Turning left would lead me into an unknown cell, but turning right would allow me to move forward into a floor cell (.), which is safe.

I will turn right and then move forward to continue my path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

</thought>
<action>

</action>
